Human CD34+ HSCs will be isolated by FACS sorting (CD34+Lin-CD38-CD90+CD45RA- for LT-HSCs, or CD34+ for total HSCs) and human MSCs (CD45-CD73+CD90+CD105+) will be isolated from bone marrow aspirates. Each sorted population (HSCs and MSCs) will be processed separately.
simt_parameters.meter: 1) Bulk RNA-seq (Illumina NovaSeq, >30M reads/sample) on sorted human HSCs and MSCs to identify differentially expressed genes (DEGs) related to inflammation, proliferation, differentiation, and lymphoid commitment pathways (e.g., STAT3, NF-kB, Wnt signaling). 2) Targeted proteomics (LC-MS/MS, Data-Independent Acquisition, DIA) on sorted human HSCs and MSCs to quantify key signaling proteins and transcription factors identified from RNA-seq and known to be involved in lymphoid differentiation and inflammatory responses. 3) Validation of key DEGs and proteins via qRT-PCR and Western Blot on a subset of samples.
simt_parameters.threshold_time: Identification of significant DEGs (FDR < 0.05, |log2FC| > 1.5) and differentially abundant proteins (p<0.05, >1.5-fold change) in HSCs and MSCs from treated DSS groups compared to vehicle/shRNA control DSS groups, indicating a shift towards a less inflammatory and more pro-lymphoid transcriptional/proteomic profile within 6 weeks. This would provide mechanistic insights supporting IH_Q_L3_M_G1_1_01 by showing how cytokine blockade/ablation directly impacts gene expression and protein activity in key progenitor cells and their niche.

l6_tasks.expected_impact: This experiment provides crucial molecular-level insights into how TNFR1/IL-6R blockade or MSC-derived cytokine ablation impacts HSC and MSC biology during inflammation. By identifying specific transcriptional and proteomic changes, it can confirm whether the interventions directly modulate pathways linked to lymphoid differentiation and inflammation, thereby strengthening the evidence for IH_Q_L3_M_G1_1_01 and providing targets for future interventions. It helps distinguish from IH_Q_L3_M_G1_1_02 (metabolic) by showing direct signaling pathway modulation.

l6_tasks.title: Comprehensive hematopoietic lineage analysis and innate immune function assessment following intervention

simt_parameters.system: Humanized NSG-SGM3 mice (Jackson Labs, 8-10 weeks old, n=10 per group) engrafted with human CD34+ HSCs and/or MSCs as described in T_L6_M_G1_1_01_A_01 and T_L6_M_G1_1_01_A_02. DSS-induced colitis model will be used. Groups include: 1) DSS + Vehicle; 2) DSS + Anti-TNFR1/IL-6R Abs; 3) DSS + MSC-shRNA control; 4) DSS + MSC-shRNA TNF-alpha/IL-6. Non-DSS controls for each intervention will also be included.
simt_parameters.intervention: Interventions will be as described in T_L6_M_G1_1_01_A_01 (antibody blockade) and T_L6_M_G1_1_01_A_02 (MSC genetic ablation). Treatments will continue for 6 weeks during DSS-induced inflammation.
simt_parameters.meter: At 6 weeks (post-inflammation): 1) Peripheral blood flow cytometry for human myeloid (CD11b+, CD14+, CD15+), erythroid (CD71+, Glycophorin A+), and megakaryocytic (CD41+, CD61+) lineages; 2) Bone marrow flow cytometry for human common myeloid progenitors (CMPs: Lin-CD34+CD38+CD123+CD45RA-), granulocyte-monocyte progenitors (GMPs: Lin-CD34+CD38+CD123+CD45RA+), and megakaryocyte-erythroid progenitors (MEPs: Lin-CD34+CD38+CD123-CD45RA-); 3) Functional assessment of human myeloid cells: Phagocytosis assay (using pHrodo E. coli bioparticles, measured by flow cytometry) and ROS production assay (using DHR123, measured by flow cytometry) on isolated human CD14+ monocytes from spleen; 4) Splenic NK cell (CD56+CD3-) cytotoxicity assay against K562 target cells (chromium release or flow cytometry-based killing assay).
simt_parameters.threshold_time: No significant reduction (p>0.05) in the frequencies or absolute counts of human myeloid, erythroid, or megakaryocytic progenitors or mature cells in peripheral blood or bone marrow in treated DSS groups compared to vehicle/shRNA control DSS groups at 6 weeks. Functional assays (phagocytosis, ROS production, NK cytotoxicity) should show no significant impairment (p>0.05) in treated DSS groups compared to controls. This would confirm that the interventions are specific to lymphoid output and do not broadly compromise other hematopoietic lineages or innate immune functions, supporting the safety and specificity required by the L4 question.

l6_tasks.expected_impact: This experiment is crucial for validating that the interventions (TNFR1/IL-6R blockade or TNF-alpha/IL-6 ablation) specifically target lymphoid output without detrimental effects on other hematopoietic lineages or innate immunity. If successful, it strengthens the argument for IH_Q_L3_M_G1_1_01 by demonstrating a targeted intervention, and mitigates concerns about broad immunosuppression, which would be a major drawback for any therapeutic strategy.

l6_tasks.title: Longitudinal assessment of adaptive immune memory formation and recall responses in treated humanized mice

simt_parameters.system: Humanized NSG-SGM3 mice (Jackson Labs, 8-10 weeks old, n=10 per group) engrafted with human CD34+ HSCs and/or MSCs as described in T_L6_M_G1_1_01_A_01 and T_L6_M_G1_1_01_A_02. DSS-induced colitis model will be used. Groups include: 1) DSS + Vehicle; 2) DSS + Anti-TNFR1/IL-6R Abs; 3) DSS + MSC-shRNA control; 4) DSS + MSC-shRNA TNF-alpha/IL-6. Non-DSS controls for each intervention will also be included.
simt_parameters.intervention: Interventions will be as described in T_L6_M_G1_1_01_A_01 (antibody blockade) and T_L6_M_G1_1_01_A_02 (MSC genetic ablation). After 6 weeks of inflammation and intervention, all mice will be immunized with a model antigen (e.g., Ovalbumin, OVA) emulsified in complete Freund's adjuvant (CFA) subcutaneously. Four weeks later, mice will receive a booster immunization with OVA in incomplete Freund's adjuvant (IFA). Two weeks after the booster, mice will be challenged with Vaccinia virus (WR strain) as in T_L6_M_G1_1_01_A_01.
simt_parameters.meter: 1) Anti-OVA antibody titers (IgG1, IgG2a) in serum via ELISA at 2 weeks post-prime, 2 weeks post-boost, and 7 days post-viral challenge; 2) OVA-specific T-cell responses in splenocytes via IFN-gamma ELISpot and intracellular cytokine staining (ICS) for CD4+ and CD8+ T cells (IFN-gamma, TNF-alpha, IL-2) 2 weeks post-boost; 3) Flow cytometry for human memory T-cell (CD45RO+CCR7+/-) and B-cell (CD27+IgD-) populations in spleen and lymph nodes 2 weeks post-boost and 7 days post-viral challenge; 4) Viral titers in spleen and liver 7 days post-challenge via plaque assay.
simt_parameters.threshold_time: Anti-OVA antibody titers and OVA-specific T-cell responses (ELISpot spots, ICS frequency) in treated DSS groups should not be significantly different (p>0.05) from vehicle/shRNA control DSS groups, and ideally recover to at least 80% of non-DSS control levels. Memory T-cell and B-cell frequencies should also be comparable. Viral titers in treated DSS groups should not be significantly higher (p>0.05) than vehicle/shRNA control DSS groups or non-DSS controls 7 days post-challenge. This would confirm that the interventions do not impair the ability to form or recall adaptive immune memory, addressing the 'without impairing systemic immune responses' clause of the L4 question.

l6_tasks.expected_impact: This experiment directly addresses the critical requirement that interventions should not impair systemic immune responses, particularly adaptive memory. If the treated groups show preserved or improved memory responses and viral clearance, it strongly supports the viability of targeting TNFR1/IL-6R or their production for immune rejuvenation, aligning with the project's goal of maintaining robust, broad-spectrum pathogen resistance. Failure would indicate a need to refine the intervention strategy.
